text: Four 8mm robotic trocars are positioned, and the robot is docked from the patient's left side. Dissection begins at the sacral promontory, with visible dissection of the right side of the peritoneum while preserving the autonomic nerves.

The surgeon enters the rectovaginal plane and continues dissection on the anterior side of the mesorectum down to the pelvic floor. Vaginal digital examination is performed. The rectum is mobilized ventrally and dissected off the posterior wall of the vagina. A 10x3 cm polypropylene mesh is inserted, with the first two stitches anchoring the posterior vaginal wall to the lower end of the mesh.

The surgeon fixes the ventrally mobilized rectum to the sacral promontory using polypropylene mesh secured with three O non-absorbable sutures. The peritoneum is then closed with three OV-lock sutures, leaving the mesh extra-peritoneal to prevent complications, thus finalizing the operation.
